description: Shared Japanese collection schema types for reusable sentence-unit based content models.

sentenceChunk.description: A learner reading unit inside a sentence unit. Chunks keep Japanese surface text, a short English rendering, and lookup references.

fields.label: Chunk Japanese
fields.description: Japanese surface text for this chunk exactly as it appears.

fields.label: English
fields.description: Short learner-friendly English meaning or paraphrase.

fields.label: References
fields.description: Lookup keys for content words or grammar patterns used in this chunk.

sentenceUnit.description: A reusable analyzable sentence or lyric line that can optionally be chunked in a later pass.

fields.label: Japanese
fields.description: Japanese sentence or lyric line exactly as it appears in the source.

fields.label: English
fields.description: Natural English translation of the standalone sentence or lyric line.

fields.label: Notes
fields.description: Sentence-unit-specific nuance, context, lyric interpretation, or reading notes.

fields.label: Grammar Notes
fields.description: High-value grammar recognition notes relevant to this sentence unit.

fields.label: Chunks
fields.description: Ordered learner chunks inside the sentence unit. This can be added in a later enrichment pass.

fields.label: ID
fields.description: Stable unique identifier for the passage.

fields.label: Title
fields.description: Short learner-friendly title for the passage.

fields.label: Japanese
fields.description: Full paragraph or short passage in natural Japanese surface form.

fields.label: English
fields.description: Natural English translation of the full passage.

fields.label: Notes
fields.description: Passage-level notes about tone, context, or reading strategy.

fields.label: Tags
fields.description: Optional machine-friendly tags for filtering or grouping.

fields.label: Sentences
fields.description: Ordered sentence units composing the passage.

songEntry.description: Song lyrics with learner-friendly sentence-level analysis.

fields.label: ID
fields.description: Stable unique identifier for the song.

fields.label: Title
fields.description: Title of the song.

fields.label: Artist
fields.description: Artist or band who performs the song.

fields.label: Album
fields.description: Album name the song is from.

fields.label: Release Year
fields.description: Year the song was released.

fields.label: Genre
fields.description: Genre or genres of the song.

fields.label: Japanese
fields.description: Full song lyrics in Japanese.

fields.label: English
fields.description: English translation of the song lyrics.

fields.label: Notes
fields.description: Song-level notes about context, cultural background, or themes.

fields.label: Tags
fields.description: Optional machine-friendly tags for filtering or grouping.

fields.label: Lines
fields.description: Ordered sentence units or lyric lines composing the song.

wordEntry.description: Japanese vocabulary entry keyed by canonical headword form.

fields.label: Kanji
fields.description: Canonical dictionary/headword kanji form if one exists, EVEN IF it is rare or not commonly used in daily writing. This field represents the linguistically recognized kanji spelling, not the most common display form. If no established kanji exists, use the standard kana/katakana headword instead.

fields.label: Reading
fields.description: The pronunciation, written in kana (usually hiragana; katakana ok for katakana words). Examples: あくま, ありがとう, どきどき / ドキドキ.

fields.label: Meaning
fields.description: English gloss/translation. Keep it short; add nuance in example notes when needed.

fields.label: Type
fields.description: Grammatical classification: how it behaves in sentences. Use `lexicalClass` to capture families like onomatopoeia or greetings that can cross POS. Rules of thumb: if it is one lexical word, pick noun/verb/adj/adverb/etc. If it is multi-word or a fixed chunk, pick expression/phrase/sentence types.

values.noun: Standalone noun (名詞). Can take particles like が/を/に. Example: 本, 勉強.
values.proper-noun: Proper noun (固有名詞): specific names of people/places/organizations (e.g., 太郎, 東京, 日本, アメリカ, ユニクロ). Use for names you generally don’t want treated as normal vocab.
values.morpheme: Bound morpheme used mainly inside compounds; not typically a standalone word (e.g., 〜学, 〜性).
values.ichidan-verb: 一段動詞 dictionary form (e.g., 食べる, 見る).
values.godan-verb: 五段動詞 dictionary form (e.g., 書く, 泳ぐ).
values.irregular-verb: Irregular verbs (する, 来る) and irregular verb entries.
values.i-adjective: い-adjective (い形容詞) (e.g., 高い, 忙しい).
values.na-adjective: な-adjective (な形容詞) (e.g., 便利, きれい).
values.particle: Particle / function word (e.g., は, が, を, に, で, から, まで).
values.adverb: Adverb (副詞) or adverb-like word used to modify verbs/clauses (e.g., すぐ, ゆっくり). Many mimetics belong here (ドキドキ(と)する, キラキラ(と)光る) — mark those with lexicalClass='onomatopoeia'.
values.counter: Counter word used with numerals (e.g., 〜人, 〜枚, 〜つ).
values.expression: Fixed conventional phrase (often multi-word) that is used as a unit (e.g., おつかれさま, しょうがない). Not a full sentence unless it clearly contains its own predicate.
values.numeral: Numeral / number-word entry used as a standalone numeric form or numeric symbol (e.g., 一, 二, 三, 百, 千, 万).
values.descriptive-phrase: Descriptive lexicalized phrase or quantified descriptive chunk that behaves as a fixed expression rather than a single simple lexical item.
values.phrase-sentence: Multi-word phrase or sentence-like chunk learned as a unit, often containing its own predicate or argument structure.
values.conjunction: Clause-linking connective/conjunction used to connect statements or explain consequence (e.g., なので).

fields.label: Semantic Class
fields.description: Semantic category/class of the word. Use plain text so it can scale across POS; examples: food, city, onomatopoeia, greeting. Use null when not needed.

fields.label: Tags
fields.description: Optional machine-friendly tags for filtering/grouping. Use short snake_case strings. Examples: fractional_numeral, jlpt_n3, grammar_marker, formal, casual, written, spoken, counter_related.
